SignOut terminates user session.

UpdateProfile updates user's first name and last name.
Validates a one-time activationToken and returns 5-min living stateToken

Retrieves all advisors a user is entitled to according to the tier.
Retrieves all alert rule templates a user is entitled to according to the tier.
Retrieves all check scripts a user is entitled to according to the tier.

Connect PMM Server to organization.
Disconnect PMM Server from organization.
Connect PMM Server to organization.
Onboards a user to Platform.
Search organization entitlements. This method only returns information if the organization is linked to an existing Percona Customer Portal account.
Search all PMM servers connected to an organization.
Invite users to the organization and specify their member roles.

message.description: Message to elaborate the reason of action failure or details of successful completion.

ActionStatusMessageActionStatus.description: ActionStatus represents a result of an action.

ActionStatusMessageActionStatusCode.description:
ActionStatusCode codifies result of an action.

 - UNSPECIFIED: UNSPECIFIED for non-passed value or ambiguous result of the operation.
 - SUCCESS: SUCCESS represents SUCCESS action status.
 - FAILURE: FAILURE represents FAILURE action status.

ActionTypeMessageActionType.description:
ActionType represents audit events' action types.

 - UNSPECIFIED: UNSPECIFIED represents unknown action type.
 - CREATE: CREATE represents action type = CREATE.
 - UPDATE: UPDATE represents action type = UPDATE.
 - DELETE: DELETE represents action type = DELETE.
 - LOG_IN: LOG_IN represents action type = LOG_IN.
 - LOG_OUT: LOG_OUT represents action type = LOG_OUT.
 - CHANGE_PASSWORD: CHANGE_PASSWORD represents action type = CHANGE_PASSWORD.

tos.title: Flag shows if the Terms of Service are accepted

marketing.title: Flag shows if the Marketing consent is given

username.description: Percona Portal username to invite to the Organziation.

role.description: Percona Portal Organization Role to be granted to invited user.

username.title: Percona Portal username that was attempted to invite to the Organization

securityAdvisor.description: Flag indicates that security advisors are covered by this entitlement.

configAdvisor.description: Flag indicates that config advisors are covered by this entitlement.

SearchMembersRequestUserFilter.description: Optional filter that PMM uses to get information about the current user.

name.description:
Name of the field (name of the column when applicable to SQL).
If a filter exists then the name field must not be empty since that
can result in an invalid SQL query and lead to non-descriptive errors.

filterType.description: filter_type defines logic of the filter.

field.description: field defines based on which data filtering must occur.

fspFilter.description: Filter is used to filter results for endpoints that return list of values.

fspFilterType.description:
FilterType represents type of filter that will be applied to SQL query.

 - EQUAL: In case single value is provided, EQUAL will trigger "=" SQL behaviour.
If array of values provided, EQUAL will trigger "IN" SQL behaviour.
 - NOT_EQUAL: In case single value is provided, NOT EQUAL will trigger "<>" SQL behaviour.
If array of values provided, EQUAL will trigger "NOT IN" SQL behaviour.
 - GREATER: GREATER is mapped to SQL '>'.
 - GREATER_OR_EQUAL: GREATER_OR_EQUAL is mapped to SQL '>='.
 - LESS: LESS is mapped to SQL '<'.
 - LESS_OR_EQUAL: LESS_OR_EQUAL is mapped to SQL '<='.

filters.description: filters are optional. No filters will be applied if absent. If multiple provided - will be joined using AND logic.

sortingParams.description: sorting_params are optional. No sorting will be applied if absent.

pageParams.description: page_params are optional. No pagination will be applied if absent.

fspFilteringSortingPagination.description: FilteringSortingPagination aggregates params for filtering, sorting and pagination.

pageSize.description: Maximum number of results per page.

index.description: Index of the requested page, starts from 0.

fspPageParams.description: PageParams represents page request parameters for pagination.

fspPageTotals.description: PageTotals represents total values for pagination and are included in paginated responses.

fspSortingOrder.description:
- ASC: ASC is for ascending sorting order.
 - DESC: DESC is for descending sorting order.

fspSortingParams.description: SortingParams are used to sort results for endpoints that return list of values.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

protobufNullValue.description:
`NullValue` is a singleton enumeration to represent the null value for the
`Value` type union.

 The JSON representation for `NullValue` is JSON `null`.

 - NULL_VALUE: Null value.

time.description: Time when this event was received by the sender.

event.description: Original event with a well-known type URL.

reporterv1Event.description: Event contains original event and additional information added by the sender.

v1AccountIDs.description: ServiceNowAccountIDs lists IDs of an account (org) in different systems.

typeUrl.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message.

binary.description:
Must be a valid serialized protobuf binary of the above specified type.
Only binary or json field must be set.

json.description:
Must be a valid serialized protobuf JSON of the above specified type.
Only binary or json field must be set.

v1AnyEvent.description:
AnyEvent represents a serialized telemetry event from the events sub-packages.
It is similar to any.Any, but:
* is not a well-known type, so no special support from the library (it is a good thing in our case);
* may contain a JSON variant of serialization.

oktaUserId.description: User's okta ID is an ID that user has in Okta.

requestId.description: Unique ID assigned to each request.

organizationId.description: Organization ID that affected object belongs to.

eventSource.description: The source module/service that triggered audit event (might be equal to account_id for events not related to user action).

objects.description: The information about affected objects in the system.

initialState.description: Object's initial attribute states that have been affected by user.

resultState.description: Object's resulted attribute states that have been affected by user.

v1AuditObject.description: AuditObject represents object with it's attributes that is affected by user action.

pmmServerUrl.description: PMM Server address accessible to users.

customerSuccess.description: Contact information of the Customer Success employee assigned to a customer's account.

v1CreateHookEventRequest.description: CreateHookEventRequest to be used to store okta event in persistent storage.

v1DistributionMethod.description:
DistributionMethod represents PMM Server distribution method.

 - DISTRIBUTION_METHOD_INVALID: DISTRIBUTION_METHOD_INVALID represents unknown distribution method.
 - DOCKER: DOCKER represents Docker distribution method.
 - OVF: OVF represents Open Virtualization Format / Open Virtual Appliance distribution method.
 - AMI: AMI represents Amazon Machine Image distribution method.
 - AZURE: AZURE represents Azure distribution method.
 - DO: DO represents Digital Ocean distribution method.

file.description: List of advisors in YAML format.
List of alert rule templates in YAML format.
List of advisor checks in YAML format.

createdAt.description: Timestamp at which the cluster was created.

dailyLimitEndsAt.description:
Timestamp at which the user can create another cluster.
This value is created_at + 24 hours.

pmmDemoUrl.description: Public address of the PMM instance created in the cluster.

failed.description:
Indicates if the cluster creation has failed.
It would be more flexible to have this field as an enum that shows the process health (InProgress, Failed, etc),
but since it's not clear how long will the feature stay, decided to stick with the bool value.

ServerMetrics contains PMM Server metrics.
There are mandatory metrics and optional metric pairs.
The exact metrics pairs (metric name, metric value) is defined by PMM Server.
